skill.name: conductor-setup
skill.description: Guides an interactive Conductor setup and creates project documentation for product, workflow, tech stack, and style decisions.
skill.summary: Set up Conductor project artifacts through structured Q&A and local documentation generation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external-command and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found these are Markdown filenames, fenced examples, headings, and ordinary prose rather than executable code or cryptographic APIs. The skill does intentionally read project indicators and create or update Conductor files, so the remaining risk is controlled filesystem modification inside the project workspace.

medium_findings.title: Project Filesystem Modification Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to create Conductor documentation files and update setup_state.json. This is expected behavior for a setup skill, but it modifies the local project workspace and should be reviewed before use on sensitive repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow directly requires file creation and state updates under conductor/. I found no evidence of destructive commands or writes outside the project, so the risk is moderate rather than high.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer treated backticked filenames, tool names, and fenced examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. Review found documentation text and Markdown formatting, not executable Ruby, shell code, or command substitution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain Markdown instructions or examples. No code path executes the backticked text as a shell or Ruby command.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts map to prose, headings, Markdown file extensions, or suggested answer text. I found no hashing, encryption, cipher selection, password storage, or security library use in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain documentation structure and setup questions, not cryptographic implementation. The absence of code or crypto APIs makes the high static severity unsupported.

content.value_statement: Project setup often leaves decisions scattered across chats and files. This skill guides structured Q&A and creates Conductor documentation for product, workflow, stack, and style decisions.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether a conductor directory or setup state file already exists.
Classifies projects as greenfield or brownfield from common project indicators.
Runs an interactive Q&A flow for product, guidelines, stack, workflow, and style guides.
Generates Conductor documentation files such as product, tech stack, workflow, tracks, and index pages.
Maintains setup progress in setup_state.json so interrupted setup can resume.
Offers regeneration options when expected Conductor files are missing.
limitations: It requires user answers before completing each setup section.
It modifies local project files and should be used in a version-controlled workspace.
It does not validate generated documentation against external product requirements.
It references optional style guide templates that must exist in the expected plugin location.

use_cases.title: Initialize a New Product Repository
use_cases.description: Create the first product, workflow, and stack documents before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Document an Existing Codebase
use_cases.description: Detect current project indicators and convert them into clear Conductor reference artifacts.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Delivery Rules
use_cases.description: Capture TDD, commit, review, verification, and style guide decisions in shared project files.

prompt_templates.title: Start Basic Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin a new Conductor setup in a repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor-setup to initialize this project. Ask one question at a time and create the standard Conductor artifacts.

prompt_templates.title: Resume Interrupted Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Continue from a saved setup state.
prompt_templates.prompt: Resume conductor-setup from the existing setup state. Confirm the current section, then continue with the next unanswered question.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up an Existing Codebase
prompt_templates.scenario: Use detected project files to prefill setup answers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run conductor-setup for this brownfield project. Inspect existing project indicators, summarize what you detect, and ask me to confirm missing details.

prompt_templates.title: Create Full Governance Artifacts
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate complete team workflow and style documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor-setup to produce product, guidelines, tech stack, workflow, track registry, and language style guide artifacts. Pause before each file write for confirmation.

output_examples.input: Start setup for a new web application.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks for the project name, description, target users, goals, stack choices, workflow rules, and style guide preferences.

output_examples.input: Resume a partially completed Conductor setup.
output_examples.output: The assistant loads the saved setup state, skips completed sections, verifies existing files, and continues from the next question.

output_examples.input: Finish setup after all questions are answered.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports the created Conductor artifacts and lists next steps for reviewing files and creating the first track.

best_practices: Run the skill in a clean git working tree so generated file changes are easy to review.
Answer setup questions with project-specific decisions instead of generic defaults.
Review every generated Conductor file before using it as team guidance.
anti_patterns: Do not run setup blindly in a repository with uncommitted documentation changes.
Do not treat inferred tech stack details as authoritative without confirmation.
Do not skip state verification when resuming an interrupted setup.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates Conductor documentation for product definition, product guidelines, tech stack, workflow, tracks, and optional code style guides.

faq.question: Can it be used on an existing project?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks common project indicators and asks you to confirm detected languages, frameworks, and conventions.

faq.question: Does it execute shell commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found in SKILL.md. The instructions describe reading files, asking questions, and creating documentation artifacts.

faq.question: Will it overwrite existing Conductor files?
faq.answer: It first checks for existing Conductor files and should ask whether to resume or reinitialize before proceeding.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally writes files in the project workspace, which is useful but requires review before publishing changes.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
